message: A mixed critic result with one confirmed blocking proposal and one insufficient-evidence blocking proposal incorrectly terminates as debate_no_consensus before accepting the confirmed blocker.
evidence: The terminal check fires on hasBlockingInsufficient without checking !hasBlockingConfirmed, contrary to the contract case where a confirmed blocking proposal plus a different insufficient proposal must not trigger debate_no_consensus.

message: Same-engine critic fallback selects the first registry entry instead of the resolved first reviewer.
evidence: When no different-engine agent exists, the contract requires using reviewers[0]; this code uses config.Agents[0], so --reviewer or pipeline.code_review.by selecting another same-engine reviewer gets the wrong critic.

message: Synthetic missing verdict artifact entries use verdict=insufficient_evidence instead of the required verdict value missing_verdict.
evidence: The contract says absent critic responses must be recorded with verdict value missing_verdict; the implementation writes verdict insufficient_evidence with a separate MissingVerdict boolean, breaking the artifact status contract.

message: A round with both a confirmed blocking proposal and an insufficient-evidence blocking proposal is incorrectly terminated as debate_no_consensus before accepting the confirmed blocker.
evidence: The code returns errDebateNoConsensus whenever hasBlockingInsufficient is true and no prior blockers exist, without checking !hasBlockingConfirmed, and this happens before the accept loop.

message: Same-engine critic fallback selects the first registry entry instead of reviewers[0].
evidence: When no different-engine critic exists, the contract requires using reviewers[0]'s agent; this code uses config.Agents[0], which is wrong when --reviewer or pipeline.code_review.by selects another same-engine entry.

message: Missing critic verdicts are recorded with the wrong artifact verdict and no warning.
evidence: Synthetic missing responses are written as verdict insufficient_evidence with missing_verdict=true, but the contract requires verdict value missing_verdict and a warning in the round summary.

message: Zero-valued precision counters disappear from loop-summary.json and --json output.
evidence: precision_confirmed, precision_rejected, and precision_unresolved all use omitempty, so required per-round critic fields are omitted whenever their count is zero.

message: The critic prompt does not allow explicit insufficient_evidence verdicts.
evidence: The prompt rules only permit confirmed or disputed and instruct uncertain critics to omit entries, while the contract requires the critic to mark insufficient_evidence and name what is missing.

message: The tests do not cover the required mixed-verdict case where one blocking proposal is confirmed and a different blocking proposal is insufficient_evidence.
evidence: The suite covers single confirmed and single insufficient cases, but no test constructs both in the same round. This gap is material because review_loop.go returns debate_no_consensus whenever hasBlockingInsufficient is true, before accepting confirmed findings.

message: The critic read-only execution requirement is not tested.
evidence: criticTestTransport.Run records only PromptRepoPath and never asserts or stores req.ReadOnly; rg found no ReadOnly assertions in review_critic_test.go, despite the contract requiring critic attempts to be tested as read-only.

message: The intra-batch dedup test contains a no-op assertion for the candidate-count behavior it claims to verify.
evidence: If the stdout check fails, the branch only assigns `_ = got` and does not fail the test, so the test would still pass without proving precision_candidates=1 or that only one duplicate survivor reached the critic.

message: The code-review-only critic_by setting is modeled on the shared pipelineStage, so critic_by is accepted under every pipeline stage even though only pipeline.code_review uses it.
evidence: CriticBy is declared on pipelineStage at lines 87-89, which all stages share, while validatePipeline only checks it inside ns.name == "code_review" at lines 299-302. With KnownFields enabled, misplaced critic_by keys on other stages become accepted but ignored no-op config.

message: The active agent configuration docs do not mention the new `pipeline.code_review.critic_by` registry-name override, so users cannot discover how to configure the critic agent.
evidence: `docs/agents.md` lists `--agent`, `--reviewer`, and `pipeline.code_review.by` as registry references, while `internal/app/config.go:87` adds `critic_by` and `internal/app/config.go:299` validates `pipeline.code_review.critic_by`.

message: The review-run docs are stale for the new critic pass: they still describe direct proposal acceptance and omit the new critic terminal reasons and round-summary fields.
evidence: `docs/flow.md` says reviewer proposals are accepted into findings and its terminal-reason list omits the new values; `internal/app/review_loop.go:105` adds critic summary fields and `internal/app/review_loop.go:659` maps `critic_verdicts_unparsed`, `debate_no_consensus`, and `precision_rejected`.
